title: veridict compare report
description: JSON output of `veridict compare` for a single --metric run. A multi-metric run instead produces multi-report.schema.json (a Vec of this shape plus an overall verdict) - see report.rs's Report/MultiReport split.

schema_version.description: Stays 1 across additive changes (new fields/variants); bumps only when a field is removed or renamed.

validity.description: Valid unless a --max-timeouts/--max-crashes/--max-invalid cap was breached, in which case verdict above is also forced to inconclusive - see FailureCaps in the Rust API.

promotion.description: The single go/no-go field: promoted only when validity is valid and verdict is pass.

effect.description: Point estimate. Centered on 0 for winrate/sign-test/elo (deviation from a 50/50 split); in the input's own units for mean-diff/quantile-diff.

estimated_additional_trials.description: Rough estimate of additional trials to reach a decisive verdict. null when already decided, zero trials, or the effect is inside the pass/fail threshold band (see docs/metrics.md). Exact (binary-searched against the real CI function) for winrate/sign-test/elo; an O(1/sqrt(n)) heuristic with a documented bias for mean-diff/quantile-diff.

warnings.description: Human-readable data-quality flags; never affects verdict. Structured counterpart is data_quality.

quantile.description: The quantile quantile-diff measured (e.g. 0.95 for p95). Present only for metric quantile-diff; omitted entirely (not null) for every other metric.

cluster_count.description: --cluster-by-id only (winrate/elo): number of distinct resampling clusters. Omitted entirely (not null) when --cluster-by-id wasn't requested.

max_cluster_size.description: --cluster-by-id only: the largest single cluster's record count.

effective_sample_size.description: --cluster-by-id only: paired_count deflated by design_effect (Kish 1965) - how many truly independent trials this clustered data is actually worth.

design_effect.description: --cluster-by-id only: Var(cluster bootstrap) / Var(i.i.d. bootstrap) on the same pooled data. 1.0 means no measurable clustering effect.

correction_method.description: Present only when `compare --claim-correction bonferroni|holm` (or its deprecated --correction alias) was requested for this run; omitted entirely (not null) otherwise, so a default run's JSON is byte-identical to before this field existed.

family_size.description: Number of --metric flags in this run's family; present only alongside correction_method. A family containing mean-diff/quantile-diff (no closed-form CI to correct against) or run with --cluster-by-id is rejected outright as a configuration error instead of being counted with a partial guarantee.

achieved_alpha.description: Smallest one-sided significance at which this report's own CI would still pass, in the same units as (1 - confidence). Present only for a report whose own verdict was pass.

adjusted_alpha_threshold.description: The corrected significance threshold achieved_alpha was actually compared against (alpha/family_size for bonferroni; alpha/(family_size-rank+1) for holm). Present exactly when achieved_alpha is.

unadjusted_verdict.description: Deprecated compatibility alias for verdict, present only alongside correction_method. Before 0.14.0, verdict itself became the *adjusted* value once correction ran, and this field held the pre-correction one alongside it; verdict is never adjusted now, so this always just equals it. New consumers should read verdict directly - see family_adjusted_verdict for the actual corrected claim. Scheduled for removal alongside a future schema_version bump.

family_adjusted_verdict.description: This metric's verdict, read as one claim among the family's simultaneous per-metric claims: a pass downgraded to inconclusive if it doesn't clear its corrected significance threshold, otherwise the same value as verdict. Computed after any FailureCaps have already been applied. Present only alongside correction_method; verdict above is never adjusted by correction.

family_adjusted_promotion.description: Promotion::decide(validity, family_adjusted_verdict) - whether this metric's improvement claim survives being considered alongside the rest of the family, separate from promotion above (the deployment gate, never touched by correction). Present exactly when family_adjusted_verdict is.

high_failure_rate.description: over 20% of trials failed to execute

draw_heavy.description: elo only; always false for other metrics. Over 50% draws.

low_id_diversity.description: one id repeated >= 3 times among >= 10 id-tagged trials, unpaired mode only; always false when --paired-by-id is set or too few records carry an id

thin_quantile_tail.description: quantile-diff only; always false for other metrics. Fewer than 10 expected observations (paired_count * min(quantile, 1-quantile)) in the thinner tail.
